title: Epic: BD Podcast Distribution Pipeline (Phase 1)
description:
Business Decisions Phase 1: automated podcast-to-social-media pipeline.
SKILL-DRIVEN ARCHITECTURE: NO AI API keys. All AI thinking runs in user's Claude Code subscription via skills. Server endpoints are RECEIVERS (validate + save), never CALLERS of AI.
7 steps: Transcribe (Whisper) -> Select Clips (SKILL) -> Cut Clips (ffmpeg) -> Generate Metadata (SKILL) -> Distribute (Upload-Post) -> Collect Analytics -> Generate Insights (SKILL).
Steps 2, 4, 7 are skill-driven: Claude Code reads data, thinks, POSTs results to server endpoints.
Architecture: Step-based workflows, PostgreSQL source of truth, R2 object storage, Upload-Post for posting.
Each step = independent feature folder + API endpoint + DB state transition.
13 accounts across TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, X, YouTube.
Target: <2 hours from recording to all posts distributed.
close_reason: Epic complete. All 22 beads closed. SKILL-DRIVEN ARCHITECTURE: zero AI API keys. 7 pipeline steps (4 providers, 7 feature endpoints, 5 Claude Code skills, 1 seed script). 138+ tests pass, tsc clean. Ready for Phase 1 dogfooding — run /process-episode to process first podcast.

Phase 3 (platform mode): pauses at approval gates.

title: Add A/B headline variant logic to landing route
description:
Add A/B headline variant logic using cookie persistence.

WHAT: Update landing page route for headline variant selection.

WHY: 4 headline variants for A/B testing per PRD.

NOTE: The PRD mentions localStorage but we use server-side cookie instead because:
- Landing page is SSR (no client JS) -- localStorage requires JS
- Cookie is sent on every request, server reads it directly
- httpOnly cookie is more secure than localStorage

IMPLEMENTATION in features/(life)/landing/routes.ts:
```tsx
import { getCookie, setCookie } from 'hono/cookie'

const VARIANTS = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'] as const
type Variant = (typeof VARIANTS)[number]

landingRoutes.get('/', (c) => {
  // Priority: URL param > cookie > random
  let variant = c.req.query('v') as Variant | undefined

  if (!variant || !VARIANTS.includes(variant)) {
    variant = getCookie(c, 'lp_variant') as Variant | undefined
  }

  if (!variant || !VARIANTS.includes(variant)) {
    variant = VARIANTS[Math.floor(Math.random() * VARIANTS.length)]
  }

  // Persist for 30 days
  setCookie(c, 'lp_variant', variant, {
    maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30,
    path: '/',
    httpOnly: true,
    sameSite: 'Lax',
  })

  return c.html(renderPage(<LandingPage variant={variant} />, {
    title: 'The Right Decision -- Life Decisions Course',
    description: 'A methodology + AI that turns stuck goals into clear decisions. $197/year. 7-day guarantee.',
  }))
})
```

SEO TITLE/DESC (from PRD Section 5):
- title: "The Right Decision -- Life Decisions Course"
- description: "A methodology + AI that turns stuck goals into clear decisions. $197/year. 7-day guarantee."

FILES:
- MODIFY features/(life)/landing/routes.ts

ACCEPTANCE:

title: Make transcription async with background processing
description:
processTranscription() returns immediately after transitioning to 'transcribing'. Actual transcription runs in background. This prevents HTTP timeouts for long videos (3-10 min).

BACKGROUND: whisper-cpp takes 3-10 min for a 30-minute video. The current implementation is synchronous: POST /api/pipeline-runs/:id/process downloads, transcribes, and returns. If the HTTP request times out (default 2 min), the server returns 504 but whisper-cpp keeps running, leaving the DB in a stale 'transcribing' state.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- MODIFY features/(business)/transcribe/service.ts:
  - Split processTranscription() into two parts:
    1. Synchronous: validate state, transition to 'transcribing', return 202
    2. Background: processInBackground() does download -> whisper -> update DB
  - Background task catches ALL errors and calls failPipeline() on failure
  - Background task cleans up temp files in finally block
- MODIFY features/(business)/transcribe/routes.ts:
  - POST /:id/process returns 202 Accepted (not 200)
  - Response body: { run: { id, status: 'transcribing' } }
- MODIFY features/(business)/transcribe/service.test.ts:
  - Test that processTranscription returns immediately with status 'transcribing'
  - Test that background task updates status to 'transcribed' on success
  - Test that background task calls failPipeline on whisper error
  - Test that temp files are cleaned up on both success and failure

ASYNC PATTERN:
  export async function processTranscription(runId: string) {
    const found = await findRunInState(runId, 'queued')
    if ('error' in found) return found
    if (!(await transitionPipeline(runId, found.run.status, 'transcribing', { startedAt: new Date() }))) {
      return { error: 'PIPELINE_INVALID_STATE' as const }
    }
    // Fire-and-forget
    processInBackground(runId, found.run.inputVideoUrl)
      .catch(err => failPipeline(runId, 'transcribe', String(err)))
    return { run: { id: runId, status: 'transcribing' as const } }
  }

TIMEOUT CONTRACT: The orchestrator skill polls GET /api/pipeline-runs/:id with a 15-minute max timeout. If still 'transcribing' after 15 min, the skill reports a timeout and offers to check again or mark as failed.

NOTE: The same async pattern should later be applied to clip-cut (ffmpeg, 30s+ per clip). For Phase 1, clip-cut can remain synchronous since individual clips are fast. Consider making clip-cut async if processing >10 clips.

IMPORTS:
- import { failPipeline } from '@/features/(business)/workflow/transitions'
- All other existing imports remain

BUILD ORDER: Step 6 (code).

REUSE CHECK:
- [x] Uses existing transitionPipeline() for state change
- [x] Uses new failPipeline() for error handling (from bead .3)
- [x] Uses existing download() and transcribe() providers

ACCEPTANCE:
- POST /process returns 202 immediately (not 200)
- Response: { run: { id, status: 'transcribing' } }
- Background task updates to 'transcribed' on success
- Background task calls failPipeline() on any error
- Temp files cleaned up in finally block (success and failure)
- Tests verify: immediate return, background success, background failure, cleanup

title: Create /process-episode orchestrator skill
description:
Claude skill that orchestrates the full 7-step pipeline by calling each API endpoint in sequence.

BACKGROUND: This is the primary interface for running the pipeline. Henry uses it from Claude Code, Indy from Claude Cowork. The skill calls each step's API, polls async steps, reports progress, handles errors, and supports resume from failed state.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- MODIFY .claude/skills/process-episode/SKILL.md -- implement the full orchestration logic

PRE-FLIGHT CHECKS (before starting pipeline):
1. Call GET /api/pipeline/health. If any provider status is 'down', report which and STOP.
2. Validate input video exists in R2: call GET /api/pipeline-runs with the video URL and check if accessible (or try a HEAD request via the storage provider).
3. Check platformAccounts: call GET or query to verify at least one platform account exists. If none, suggest running /api/platform-accounts/sync first.
4. If ALL checks pass, proceed. If any fail, report exactly what's wrong and stop.

PIPELINE FLOW:
1. TRANSCRIBE: POST /api/pipeline-runs { videoUrl, config: { dryRun } } -> POST /:id/process (returns 202) -> poll GET /:id every 5s until status is 'transcribed' or 'failed' (15 min max timeout)
2. CLIP SELECT: GET /:id to read transcript. Display transcript to user. Prompt for clip selections (start/end timestamps, score, title). POST /api/clip-select with selections. (Phase 2: AI does this automatically)
3. CLIP CUT: POST /api/clip-cut/:runId. Wait for response (synchronous for Phase 1). Report success/failure per clip.
4. METADATA: Prompt user for metadata per clip per platform (description, hashtags, CTA). POST /api/metadata-generate. (Phase 2: AI does this automatically)
5. DISTRIBUTE: POST /api/post-distribute/:runId. If dryRun, report 'DRY RUN: posts logged but not sent.' Otherwise report posted count.
6. ANALYTICS: If not dry-run AND posts are older than 24h, POST /api/analytics-collect. Otherwise skip with note 'Analytics skipped (posts too recent or dry-run).'
7. SUMMARY: GET /api/pipeline-runs/:id for final status. Display per-step timing from stepTimings. Show total duration. Report clips posted, clips failed.

RESUME LOGIC:
- On start, check if a run ID was provided (resume mode) or a video URL (new run).
- If resume: GET /api/pipeline-runs/:id, check current status, skip completed steps, resume from current state.
- Example: if status is 'cut', skip transcribe/clip-select/clip-cut, go straight to metadata.
- The state machine supports failed -> any processing state, so failed runs can retry the specific step.

ERROR HANDLING:
- If any step returns an error, display: 'Step [N] ([name]) failed: [error code] -- [message]'
- Offer options: (A) Retry this step (B) Skip to next step (C) Abort pipeline
- If retry: call the same step API again
- If skip: note the skip and continue (some steps may fail downstream)

BUILD ORDER: Step 8 (wire into skills -- last step, depends on all APIs being ready).

ACCEPTANCE:
- New run: /process-episode episodes/test.mp4 runs all 7 steps
- Resume: /process-episode --resume lyon-xxx picks up from failed step
- Pre-flight blocks if any provider is down
- Pre-flight blocks if no platform accounts configured
- Async steps (transcribe) polled with 5s interval, 15 min timeout
- Errors show step name + error code + retry option
- Summary shows per-step timing
- Dry-run mode is respected (no actual posting)
- Works from both Claude Code and Claude Cowork
close_reason: Implemented full orchestrator skill with pre-flight checks, async polling, dry-run support, resume from failed step, per-step error handling, and summary report. Phase 1 uses manual clip selection + metadata, Phase 2 will automate via AI.

title: Implement PostHog event taxonomy — 15 core events

COURSE: course_module_started (module_id, module_name), course_module_completed (module_id, time_spent), exercise_started (exercise_id, exercise_type), exercise_completed (exercise_id, time_spent)
PAYMENT: checkout_started (plan, price), checkout_completed (plan, price, payment_method), subscription_cancelled (plan, reason, lifetime_value)
CONTENT: content_uploaded (type, size), content_processed (type, duration), content_distributed (platform, status)
ERROR: error_occurred (error_code, path, stack) — NOTE: this is handled by .12 (error middleware), skip here
NAVIGATION: page_viewed (path, referrer, device) — NOTE: this is handled by PostHog autocapture in .10, skip here

Naming convention: noun_verb_past_tense

WHY: Can't measure what you don't track. These events power the 3 key funnels (acquisition, activation, revenue).
